title: Caucasian Multi-Stripe Runner with Polychrome Geometric Panels
lead: A narrow runner organized in vertical stripes of differing character: a deep madder field packed with interlocking S-scroll octagons alternates with a blue-ground panel of small diamond lattice, flanked by ivory minor borders carrying repeating cruciform and star figures.

designFeatures: interlocking S-scroll octagons on madder ground
diamond lattice panel on blue ground
repeating cruciform figures in ivory borders
distinguishing: Unusually pronounced vertical stripe format with no horizontal medallion axis — suggests a long-format ceremonial or corridor commission
Two distinctly different all-over repeat systems placed in parallel stripes, a hallmark of certain Shirvan workshop production

title: Persian Ivory Field Medallion Rug with Floral Surround
lead: Ivory central field carries a layered medallion in deep crimson and navy, surrounded by scrolling vine and multicolor floral sprays. A wide crimson-and-blue border with repeating palmette and arabesque frames the composition on all four sides.

designFeatures: central pendant medallion with floral interior
ivory open field with scattered flowering branches
wide crimson main border with palmette and floral repeat
multiple guard stripes with small rosette chain
scalloped spandrels in crimson and blue
distinguishing: Unusually open ivory field creates strong contrast against the dense border treatment
Medallion pendant composition reads as Tabriz workshop format with balanced bilateral symmetry

title: Blue-Field Plainweave Runner in Chambray Tones
lead: An all-over plain field in soft slate-blue, with fine vertical streaking suggesting a slubbed or ikat-style weft giving gentle tonal variation. No border. Clean edges, even weave throughout, with no visible wear or repair.

distinguishing: Consistent vertical weft streaking creates subtle texture without a formal pattern
Absence of any border gives a clean, modern utility-runner quality

title: Neutral Tone Flatweave Patchwork Runner in Olive Khaki
lead: A long, narrow flatweave runner in a uniform olive-khaki ground, assembled from multiple panel sections joined with visible white running stitches. The overall field reads as near-solid with subtle tonal variation across the panels.

distinguishing: Multiple kilim panels seamed together lengthwise in traditional Turkish patchwork fashion
Joining stitches left exposed as a structural design element rather than concealed

title: Tribal Scatter Rug with Allover Geometric Field
lead: Warm taupe and charcoal field carries scattered geometric medallions, snowflake rosettes, and rectilinear frames in ivory and brown. The lower field shifts darker with an abstracted architectural register. Short ivory fringe along one end.

distinguishing: Deliberate tonal shift between upper and lower field creates a two-register composition unusual in scatter format
Mix of curvilinear rosettes and strictly rectilinear frames within a single field suggests a tribal-revival design vocabulary

title: Silver-Grey Shaggy Wool Scatter Rug Solid Field
lead: Solid slate-grey shag rug with long, loosely curled pile throughout. No border or pattern — interest comes entirely from the texture of the tufted wool, which catches light unevenly to suggest subtle depth. Edges appear clean and even.

distinguishing: Exceptionally uniform dye across the full pile length
Curl pattern in the pile creates an organic, fleece-like surface movement

title: Persian Open-Field Medallion Rug with Floral Spandrels
lead: Deep crimson open field anchored by a layered diamond medallion with ivory and navy floral surround; four-corner spandrels mirror the medallion's botanical detail; wide navy main border carries dense palmette-and-vine repeat on all four sides.

designFeatures: open crimson field with generous negative space
wide floral main border on navy ground
multiple guard stripes with geometric meander
pendant bouquet clusters at field edges
distinguishing: Unusually generous open field relative to medallion size creates rare visual breathing room
Spandrel floral clusters extend noticeably far into the field, softening the geometric structure

title: Abstract Green and Black Geometric Field Rug
lead: Lime and moss greens play against deep charcoal and near-black across an all-over small-scale geometric lattice, the pattern partially obscured by an intentional abrash-style distressing that creates irregular shadowed bands throughout the field. No formal border.

distinguishing: Deliberate erosion or 'erased' aesthetic layered over a tight geometric ground — a hallmark of the modern overdyed or abstract-wash workshop style
The vertical crease running the length of the rug is a fold artifact from storage, not a design element

title: Overdyed Persian Allover Vine-Scroll Room Rug
lead: A mid-century Persian field rug overdyed in steel blue and charcoal, retaining a dense allover vine-scroll and palmette lattice. The dark border carries paisley and floral reserves; the interior field shows intentional abrash from the overdye process.

distinguishing: Intentional overdye treatment creates high-contrast two-tone surface unusual for traditional Tabriz production
Visible fold crease along vertical center axis suggests extended storage — warrants flat-lay period before installation

title: Overdyed Navy Blue Medallion Field Carpet
lead: A heavily overdyed carpet in deep navy and steel blue, carrying a geometric medallion field derived from a classic Persian or Caucasian source weaving. Intentional abrash and controlled distressing give the surface a worn, aged character throughout.

distinguishing: Monochromatic two-tone palette achieved through full overdye process, suppressing original color range
Deliberately distressed surface mimics antique wear while structure remains intact

title: Abstract Geometric Distressed Field Teal and Charcoal
lead: A large-format contemporary field rug in deep charcoal and aged teal, carrying an all-over lattice of small interlocking circular motifs that fade in and out of a deliberately distressed, abrash-heavy ground. The worn-down surface creates organic cloud-like voids.

designFeatures: intentional wear pattern creating organic voids
tonal contrast between lattice and ground
distinguishing: Distressed finish is designed rather than aged — the wear pattern forms deliberate cloud-like negative shapes across the field
The geometric lattice motif references traditional Islamic geometric screens yet reads as fully contemporary in execution

title: Antique Persian Medallion Rug Amber and Indigo
lead: Warm amber-gold field carries a vertical sequence of ogival medallions filled with dense floral arabesques, all set against a deeply worn indigo-navy border dressed with oversized palmettes and paisley forms. Age has burnished the pile to a lustrous patina.

distinguishing: Unusually warm amber ground rather than the more common ivory or rose typical of Kirman production
Significant overall wear has created a two-tone relief effect, revealing foundation threads in places and lending an aged textile quality

title: Distressed Allover Geometric Field Green-Black Abstract
lead: A room-size rug in layered sage, moss, and near-black, carrying a fine allover geometric lattice — small interlocking circles — beneath a deliberately abraded, painterly wash of color blocks suggesting intentional distressing.

designFeatures: no formal border — edges fade to dark
distinguishing: two-layer visual effect: fine geometric ground visible beneath loose abstract color wash
color blocks read as architectural planes, unusual for a geometric-ground rug

title: Persian Medallion Carpet in Burnt Orange and Indigo
lead: Worn to a warm amber-copper ground, this large carpet carries a lobed central medallion within a field of dense arabesque scrollwork. Deep indigo borders frame the composition on all four sides, holding palmette-and-vine main border guards. Wear is even and age-consistent throughout.

distinguishing: Unusually rich copper patina suggests prolonged oxidation of original madder or synthetic red dye
High contrast indigo border remains deeply saturated relative to worn field, suggesting differential pile height originally

title: Overdyed Green-Black Vintage Persian Room Carpet
lead: A vintage Persian carpet overdyed in deep olive and black, retaining a symmetrical medallion-and-field layout beneath the saturated color. Wear through the pile in the upper and lower fields gives the piece a deliberately distressed character, with the original geometric ornament legible throughout the center.

distinguishing: Intentional overdye treatment obscures original palette while preserving pattern structure
Differential pile wear creates tonal gradient effect strongest at top and bottom fields
Classical Persian layout rendered in an unconventional monochromatic green-black scheme

title: Distressed-Ground Geometric Lattice Rug in Olive and Black
lead: Olive-green lattice field printed with interlocking geometric medallions and dense arabesque fill on a near-black ground; deliberate abrash and distressing give the surface an aged, worn quality that reads antique at distance. Narrow stepped border frames the field on all four sides.

distinguishing: Intentional wear simulation creates a convincing antique surface that obscures the underlying repeat
Two-color palette of olive and black is unusually restrained for this lattice format

title: Abstract Geometric Rose and Charcoal Field Rug
lead: Rose-blush and charcoal field carries an all-over small geometric lattice — circles within diamonds — interrupted by large abstract weathered panels suggesting deliberate distressing. The asymmetric dark passages read almost architectural, contrasting the delicate repeat.

designFeatures: no formal border — open field composition
distinguishing: Deliberate erased-pattern aesthetic blends traditional geometric repeat with abstract expressionist color blocking
Absence of a conventional border gives the field an unusually modern, canvas-like quality

title: Distressed Allover Geometric Field Rug in Charcoal
lead: Charcoal and muted mauve field carries a dense, repeating small-circle geometric lattice overlaid on a deliberately distressed ground. Worn striations and abrash-like tonal shifts create the impression of age across the entire surface.

designFeatures: open field with no formal border
distinguishing: Distressing is designed rather than natural, producing a deliberate vintage appearance
Geometric lattice pattern reads as a photographic screen or halftone when viewed at distance

title: Over-Dyed Persian Room Rug in Burnt Orange
lead: A heavily over-dyed field in burnt orange and charcoal presents an all-over floral lattice with a faint central medallion. The wide dark border carries palmette and cartouche repeats. Wear is consistent with age; pile is intact across most of the field.

distinguishing: Deliberate over-dyeing in orange unifies and partially obscures the original pattern, a technique associated with the contemporary overdyed market applied to older foundations
Contrast between the saturated field and the surviving dark charcoal border suggests the border pile is denser or was dyed differently

title: Teal-Ground Paris Skyline Eiffel Tower Pictorial Rug
lead: Deep teal and near-black ground carries a ghosted Eiffel Tower silhouette against a diffused cityscape, overlaid throughout with a fine geometric lattice of small star-and-circle repeat in pale silver-blue. Abrash-like tonal shifts from slate to aqua read as intentional distressing.

distinguishing: Rare fusion of Oriental geometric lattice superimposed over a Western architectural pictorial
Intentional distressed finish gives an antique-washed appearance to a contemporary design
